what_it_does: This file is a research synthesis document that examines the Right-Hand-Man (RHM) concept in the Company system, distinguishes built from designed-only components, and argues that the RHM could serve as the conversational layer through which Tim interacts with the supervisor via OpenWebUI, identifying a thin OpenAI-protocol adapter as the remaining gap.
observations: Defines the RHM as a layered collective cognition, not a single-model agent.
brain_router.py is described as a source-router that resolves questions to fleet, recall, or model sources.
The fleet source already reads the supervisor's live roster, sessions, and recent channel traffic, surfacing wake/consult as a gated proposal.
No OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint exists yet in bridge.py or ops/.
The RHM config defaults to deepseek-v4-pro:cloud, but brain_router overrides this with a kimi context-size pick, creating a model-resolution inconsistency across paths.
The V-overlay surface RightHand.tsx is mostly built with a 6-verb radial fan; drive and generate are stubs.
The keystone GENERATE-write direction (read -> generate -> write output) is designed-only.
Supervisor operations are split into read (safe) and consequential/gated (propose-only).
Proposes two complementary integration shapes: Shape A (RHM-as-the-model via OpenAI adapter) and Shape B (RHM-mind + supervisor tools via mcpo).
Evidence is tagged [OBS], [INF], [IDEA], with explicit note that nothing was run end-to-end (no [VER]).
standouts: The central claim: 'chat with the supervisor' is already designed as 'ask the RHM a fleet question'; the only missing piece is an OpenAI protocol adapter.
The propose-floor: the fleet source returns gated proposals and never fires consequential supervisor actions.
A model-resolution inconsistency exists between rhm_config, brain_router, and /api/decision/explain.
The unbuilt keystone click-ask change/generate write-back wire is flagged as the single missing rung.
conventions: Uses evidence tags [OBS file:line], [INF], [IDEA] and explicitly disclaims end-to-end verification.
Maintains a neutral, file-local stance with no ranking.
Structured as a research answer with lead question, method, one-paragraph answer, detailed sections, and recommendation.
Cross-references sibling research docs and design documents.
Includes a vocabulary caution distinguishing derived terms from verbatim quotes.
concerns: Model-resolution inconsistency across RHM paths could lead to different models answering depending on endpoint.
The fleet source proposal behavior is observed in code but its end-to-end payload shape is inferred, not verified.
OpenWebUI integration depends on a not-yet-built protocol adapter and unresolved inbound transport for fabric-to-Tim messages.
Designed-only features such as mind-swap modes, follow-me/piggy-back, and the RHM Walkthrough Organ are mentioned and could expand scope.
notes: A research note arguing the RHM is already the designed conversational mind for the supervisor and only needs an OpenAI-protocol adapter to become Tim's OpenWebUI chat interface, while flagging a model-resolution inconsistency and several unbuilt features.
questions: What exact fields does /api/brain/ask return, and how is the proposal object serialized?
How does /api/chat/stream emit parts, and can it be mapped cleanly to OpenAI SSE deltas?
What is the current operational status of the fleet source in production?
How would the proposed OpenAI adapter authenticate with OpenWebUI?
What is the intended resolution for the model default mismatch between rhm_config and brain_router?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to research the RHM and evaluate whether it could be the conversational layer Tim uses through OpenWebUI; the file does exactly that, plus a build recommendation and gap analysis.
apparent_themes: Layered collective cognition over single-model agent.
Read/propose safety floor for supervisor operations.
Altitude transformation (human meaning up, technical addresses down).
One mind, multiple faces (V-icon and OpenWebUI).
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to collapse the OpenWebUI integration problem into a small, existing capability by showing that the RHM already mediates supervisor access, so the missing piece is just a protocol adapter rather than a new mind.
novelty: The distinctive angle that 'chatting with the supervisor' is not a separate tool integration but already the RHM's fleet source, and that OpenWebUI should be a second face on the same mind rather than a new agent.
contribution: This file contributes a concise architectural framing that maps the RHM's existing source-router, fleet source, and propose-floor onto an OpenWebUI conversational interface, plus a prioritized gap list (OpenAI adapter, model unification, inbound transport) that a fused system would keep as design rationale.
summary_for_embedding: Research synthesis on the Right-Hand-Man (RHM) that defines it as a layered collective cognition, inventories built versus designed-only parts, and argues the RHM can be the OpenWebUI conversational layer for the supervisor with only an OpenAI-protocol adapter remaining.
intention_for_embedding: To answer whether the RHM is already the right conversational mind for Tim's OpenWebUI chat and to identify the smallest remaining build steps.
